description: Clean security-sensitive TypeScript — innerHTML to sanitized value, eval-like function name without eval, SQL pattern in comment, process.env with defaults — zero real bugs
false_positive_traps: Line 73: container.innerHTML = safeHtml — safeHtml is the output of DOMPurify.sanitize() called on line 65 with an explicit allowlist config; this is the correct safe pattern for innerHTML assignment
Line 87: evaluateTemplateString — function name contains 'evaluate' but the implementation uses String.replace() with a regex and escapeHtml() on substituted values; eval() is never called
Line 114-116: process.env accesses — all three have ?? fallback defaults; no unchecked direct access to env vars
Line 133: SQL-like SELECT statement appears only inside a JSDoc comment to explain conceptual logic — it is not executed as a query anywhere in the code
